title: Ethereal Kenyan Spinach Pilaf
description: A light, herbal Kenyan-inspired pilaf that blends fragrant sansho pepper and sautéed spinach with aromatic coconut milk, finished with a dramatic flambé touch.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, garlic, and ginger, sauté until onion is translucent.
Stir in rice and toast for 2 minutes, coating grains in oil.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable broth, bring to a simmer, then cover and reduce heat to low.
When the mixture is bubbling, sprinkle sansho powder, salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Cover the pot again and cook for 15 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
Gently fold in the fresh spinach, allowing it to wilt into the pilaf.
Remove from heat, let stand for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
For flambé, pour a small splash of white wine into the pan, ignite with a long match, and allow flames to subside.
